author: @MrAnderson4509
text:
This will be fun.... have you considered a cycle car? more work but terrifically rewarding on completion. If your curious lookup:
Cyclecar  
that should return stuff from 1910-1920's
or 
Fraser Nash
I had allot of fun going through all that historical building, engineering stuff

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I was thinking of a cycle car mate - love them myself - but we were given a scrap lawn mower which is where this really came from - but cycle car is on the list of 'to do's'

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: Use 4 small motor scooter wheels so that it can hold the weight of you and the batteries be able to hold up to the speed. You did say this is going to be a electric vehicle right. Think weight and then think weight some more. LOL Rob's thinking: Ok Luke lets keep this simple, Luke: No Dad, I want a new Tesla style 4 wheeler. Me: It is going to get heavy guys, lol - I hope you are going to use a aluminum race seat with 4 point contact seat belts harness. I don't want to see either of you getting dead on me, please think safety on this build. Especially if you plan on trying something new like brakes or speed with a lot of weight propelled in motion. I love the build so far, looks like you are having as much fun as me brother and I had when we built our first race care. I use to race at Greenville Pickens Speedway about 11 years ago. It is a 1/2 mile asphalt track. Stock Renegade class, 1975 Camaro with a 400 small block and a 4 speed Munson transmission. I could get up to 92 miles and hour before needing to hit those brakes.  That curve comes up on you quick on that track. The car itself has been up to 150 mph on a near by straight away. Anyways, have fun guys, I'm sure you know exactly what your doing, LOL - You could name it, Mad-Scientist Racing and get Luke to make some decals that say that down each side, Some midnight blue would look awesome white lettering. Oh and remember weight, weight, weight.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate this thing will do 10-15mph if you are lucky lol - it's a first effort for us - but the next one - well anything goes lol - cheers though - some really nice advice

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering LOL and your thinking 10-15 is not fast, oh wow. Maybe in a car its not fast but in a go-cart that is a broken bone speed and death if the head hits wrong. I completely understand, I own a 4 wheeler and I use to think the same way until I caught the edge of a tree one day and I was only going about 10 mph and it threw me, lucky for me a creek was what caught my fall. I'm just being mindful of your safety tips, I don't want either of you hurt. I like you guys.

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: LOL, I'm usually the one who throws causing to the wind and does all the dangerous stuff without think all the safety stuff is not for me, lol Weird to see me thinking for once about it.

author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
Honestly I think it would be interesting to mount drum brakes so they press out from the stock rim into the modified rim so it disguises the brake profile but still adds braking power.

Putting the brakes inside the rim also protects the brakes from weather related wear to a small degree.

Just a few thoughts I had chaps, looking forward to seeing it done.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice thoughts mate - cheers

replies.author: @jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering it Will be a relatively lightweight platform, the important part for it to be able to propell itself on water is that the waterline doesn't go over 50percent of the wheel as the effect if lower will cause forward and reverse at the same time, I like Russian shed built amphibious atv's :)

author: @infinitelyuniversal2390
text:
I keep seeing that Electric Car in the background, when are you going to put some awesome batteries in it? Get the wheels spinning on her again its been years already. I Love your Work!

By the way your Conductive Ink is super awesome! Extremely conductive, and sticks very well to any surface!  Just want to say Thank you, this is the one key ingredient i wanted perfect without investing time in doing it myself!, i can do alot of things but theres only so much time in a day to diy everything straight from scratch.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are right mate there is only so much time and the twit problem is ongoing with little time to spend on it - but it will get there

replies.text: I agree the conductive ink is awesome. I've been playing around with it for a while now and I keep coming up with new things to try.
